[1st Dragoons](/wiki/1st_Cavalry_Regiment_(United_States) "1st Cavalry Regiment (United States)") [2nd Infantry Regiment](/wiki/2nd_Infantry_Regiment_(United_States) "2nd Infantry Regiment (United States)") [6th Infantry Regiment](/wiki/6th_Infantry_Regiment_(United_States) "6th Infantry Regiment (United States)") [Quartermaster Corps](/wiki/Quartermaster_Corps_(United_States_Army) "Quartermaster Corps (United States Army)") [1st California Infantry](/wiki/1st_California_Infantry "1st California Infantry")
[United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") [Quechan Tribe of the Fort Yuma Indian Reservation](/wiki/Quechan#History "Quechan")

was a [fort](/wiki/Fort "Fort") in [California](/wiki/California "California") located in [Imperial County](/wiki/Imperial_County,_California "Imperial County, California"), across the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") from [Yuma, Arizona](/wiki/Yuma,_Arizona "Yuma, Arizona"). It was established in 1848. It served as a stop on the [Butterfield Overland Mail](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail "Butterfield Overland Mail") route from 1858 until 1861. The fort was retired from active military service on May 16, 1883, and transferred to the [Department of the Interior](/wiki/Department_of_the_Interior "Department of the Interior"). The Fort Yuma Indian School and the [Saint Thomas Yuma Indian Mission](/wiki/Saint_Thomas_Yuma_Indian_Mission "Saint Thomas Yuma Indian Mission") now occupy the site. It is one of the "associated sites" listed as [Yuma Crossing and Associated Sites](/wiki/Yuma_Crossing_and_Associated_Sites "Yuma Crossing and Associated Sites") on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in the [Yuma Crossing National Heritage Area](/wiki/Yuma_Crossing_National_Heritage_Area "Yuma Crossing National Heritage Area").[[2]](#cite_note-nris-2) In addition, it is registered as [California Historical Landmark](/wiki/California_Historical_Landmark "California Historical Landmark") #806.[[3]](#cite_note-CHL-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

US Army fort (1851–1853) in California near Yuma, Arizona

For the film, see [Fort Yuma (film)](/wiki/Fort_Yuma_(film) "Fort Yuma (film)").

**Fort Yuma** was a [fort](/wiki/Fort "Fort") in [California](/wiki/California "California") located in [Imperial County](/wiki/Imperial_County,_California "Imperial County, California"), across the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") from [Yuma, Arizona](/wiki/Yuma,_Arizona "Yuma, Arizona"). It was established in 1848. It served as a stop on the [Butterfield Overland Mail](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail "Butterfield Overland Mail") route from 1858 until 1861. The fort was retired from active military service on May 16, 1883, and transferred to the [Department of the Interior](/wiki/Department_of_the_Interior "Department of the Interior"). The Fort Yuma Indian School and the [Saint Thomas Yuma Indian Mission](/wiki/Saint_Thomas_Yuma_Indian_Mission "Saint Thomas Yuma Indian Mission") now occupy the site. It is one of the "associated sites" listed as [Yuma Crossing and Associated Sites](/wiki/Yuma_Crossing_and_Associated_Sites "Yuma Crossing and Associated Sites") on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in the [Yuma Crossing National Heritage Area](/wiki/Yuma_Crossing_National_Heritage_Area "Yuma Crossing National Heritage Area").[[2]](#cite_note-nris-2) In addition, it is registered as [California Historical Landmark](/wiki/California_Historical_Landmark "California Historical Landmark") #806.[[3]](#cite_note-CHL-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fort_Yuma&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Pre-Civil War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fort_Yuma&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-Civil War")]

First established after the end of the [Mexican–American War](/wiki/Mexican%E2%80%93American_War "Mexican–American War") (1848), the fort was originally located in the bottoms near the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River"), less than a mile below the mouth of the [Gila River](/wiki/Gila_River "Gila River"). It was constructed to defend the newly settled community of Yuma, [New Mexico Territory](/wiki/New_Mexico_Territory "New Mexico Territory"), located on the other side of the Colorado River, and the nearby Mexican border.

In March 1851 the post was moved to a small elevation on the Colorado's west bank. This site had been occupied by Camp Calhoun, named for Senator [John C. Calhoun](/wiki/John_C._Calhoun "John C. Calhoun"). It was established on October 2, 1849, by 1st [Lieutenant Cave J. Couts](/wiki/Cave_Johnson_Couts "Cave Johnson Couts"), 1st Dragoons, for the boundary survey party led by 2nd Lieutenant [Amiel W. Whipple](/wiki/Amiel_W._Whipple "Amiel W. Whipple"), Corps of Topographical Engineers. A ferry service, maintained by the soldiers for the survey party's convenience, also accommodated emigrants.

Fort Yuma was established during the [Yuma War](/wiki/Yuma_War "Yuma War") to protect the southern emigrant travel route to California and to attempt control of the territorial [Quechan](/wiki/Quechan "Quechan"), or Yumans, in their homeland, the surrounding 100-mile (160 km) area. Established by Captain [Samuel P. Heintzelman](/wiki/Samuel_P._Heintzelman "Samuel P. Heintzelman"), [2nd Infantry Regiment](/wiki/2nd_Infantry_Regiment_(United_States) "2nd Infantry Regiment (United States)"), the fort was originally named 'Camp Independence.'[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In March 1851, when the post was moved to its permanent site, its name was changed to **Camp Yuma**. A year later the post was designated **Fort Yuma**. In June 1851 the Army virtually abandoned the post because of the high costs incurred in maintaining it, and it was completely abandoned on December 6, 1851, when its commissary was practically empty of provisions. The post, however, was reoccupied by Captain Heintzelman on February 29, 1852.

It was difficult to supply the post during its early years. Food supplies and construction materials were shipped by water from [San Diego, California](/wiki/San_Diego,_California "San Diego, California"), around the [Baja Peninsula](/wiki/Baja_Peninsula "Baja Peninsula") and up the [Gulf of California](/wiki/Gulf_of_California "Gulf of California") to the mouth of the Colorado River at the [Colorado River Delta](/wiki/Colorado_River_Delta "Colorado River Delta") in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). The work of transferring the goods to wagons at that point and hauling them across the [Yuma Desert](/wiki/Yuma_Desert "Yuma Desert") and [Yuha Desert](/wiki/Yuha_Desert "Yuha Desert") to Yuma was rigorous and time-consuming. Life at the post was harsh and the military's resolve to maintain a [garrison](/wiki/Garrison "Garrison") here vacillated. Only after August 1852 did the temporary Camp Yuma became permanent Fort Yuma, and the Army resolved to stay for good. In November 1852 a steamboat, Uncle Sam was launched and in December began carrying a cargo up the Colorado River from [Robinson's Landing](/wiki/Robinson%27s_Landing "Robinson's Landing") it arrived at Fort Yuma and delivered thirty-two tons of goods on December 3. Steamboats continued to supply the fort and later settlements on the Colorado River in this way until the arrival of the railroad in 1877.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3f/Map_of_a_Reconnaissance_of_the_Colorado_River_From_the_Mouth_of_the_Gila_-_NARA_-_93193141.jpg/250px-Map_of_a_Reconnaissance_of_the_Colorado_River_From_the_Mouth_of_the_Gila_-_NARA_-_93193141.jpg)](/wiki/File:Map_of_a_Reconnaissance_of_the_Colorado_River_From_the_Mouth_of_the_Gila_-_NARA_-_93193141.jpg)

Map of a Reconnaissance of the Colorado River From the Mouth of the Gila by 1st Lieutenant J.L. White, 3rd U.S. Artillery, 20 January 1858 (NAID 93193141)

Only one military action occurred at the fort when Yuman warriors surrounded it during the [Yuma War](/wiki/Yuma_War "Yuma War"), temporarily trapping the future general [Thomas William Sweeny](/wiki/Thomas_William_Sweeny "Thomas William Sweeny") and a few others. The action was led by Matthew P. Coltrain. He was awarded a Medal of Honor and a statue in remembrance of his heroic actions.

From 1858 to 1861 the [Butterfield Overland Mail](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail "Butterfield Overland Mail") had a [stagecoach](/wiki/Stagecoach "Stagecoach") station near the fort, that was subsequently used by other stage lines until the advent of the railroad in 1877.

### Civil War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fort_Yuma&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Civil War")]

During the [American Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"), the Union retained control of Fort Yuma when the [First California Infantry](/wiki/1st_California_Infantry "1st California Infantry") replaced Regular Army soldiers sent East in December 1861.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The southern half of [New Mexico Territory](/wiki/New_Mexico_Territory "New Mexico Territory") seceded, becoming the [Confederate Territory of Arizona](/wiki/Confederate_Territory_of_Arizona "Confederate Territory of Arizona") until 1862 when the [California Column](/wiki/California_Column "California Column") marching from Fort Yuma expelled the Confederacy, marching as far as western [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"). In 1863 the Union established their control of the region as the [Arizona Territory](/wiki/Arizona_Territory "Arizona Territory"). Fort Yuma served as the supply point for the Union garrisons there. There was no battle action at the fort since the western United States was far removed from the Civil War.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Post-Civil War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fort_Yuma&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Post-Civil War")]

Fort Yuma was closely associated with the [Yuma Quartermaster Depot](/wiki/Yuma_Quartermaster_Depot_State_Historic_Park "Yuma Quartermaster Depot State Historic Park") on the Arizona side of the river, which provided military supplies and personnel to posts throughout Arizona and New Mexico. The Quartermaster Depot operated between 1864 and 1891, though the Army terminated most operations there eight years earlier.

The depot was used by the Army to store and distribute supplies for all military posts in Arizona, Nevada, Utah, New Mexico and Texas during the [Indian War period](/wiki/American_Indian_Wars "American Indian Wars"). A six-month supply of clothing, food, ammunition, and other goods was stored at the depot at all times. Supplies were brought from California by ocean vessels traveling around the Baja Peninsula to [Port Isabel](/wiki/Port_Isabel,_Sonora "Port Isabel, Sonora") near the mouth of the Colorado River. There, cargos were transferred to river steamers and brought upstream to Yuma.

Supplies were unloaded at the depot and hauled up a track running from the dock to a storehouse. The depot quartered up to 900 mules and crews of teamsters to handle them. The [Southern Pacific Railroad](/wiki/Southern_Pacific_Railroad "Southern Pacific Railroad") reached Yuma in 1877. There was little need for the Quartermaster Depot and Fort Yuma, and they were abandoned on May 16, 1883. The reservation was transferred to the [Interior Department](/wiki/Interior_Department "Interior Department") on July 22, 1884.

The Fort Yuma Indian School opened in 1884, operated by Presbyterians. In 1886, the [Sisters of St. Joseph of Carondelet](/wiki/Sisters_of_St._Joseph_of_Carondelet "Sisters of St. Joseph of Carondelet") took over operations of the school.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fort_Yuma&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Description")]

Fort Yuma is now part of the [Fort Yuma Indian Reservation](/wiki/Fort_Yuma_Indian_Reservation "Fort Yuma Indian Reservation"). Numerous historic buildings remain from the military period in the [Yuma Crossing and Associated Sites](/wiki/Yuma_Crossing_and_Associated_Sites "Yuma Crossing and Associated Sites") in the [Yuma Crossing National Heritage Area](/wiki/Yuma_Crossing_National_Heritage_Area "Yuma Crossing National Heritage Area"). The [Yuma Territorial Prison](/wiki/Yuma_Territorial_Prison "Yuma Territorial Prison") and [Yuma Quartermaster Depot State Historic Parks](/wiki/Yuma_Quartermaster_Depot_State_Historic_Park "Yuma Quartermaster Depot State Historic Park") are in Arizona with buildings preserved or reconstructed, and with museums. The [Yuma Proving Ground](/wiki/Yuma_Proving_Ground "Yuma Proving Ground") is the lineal Army descendant of these original military posts in the Yuma area.

## Historic structures

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fort_Yuma&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Historic structures")]

The following images are of the historic structures in Fort Yuma.

Historic Fort Yuma  
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/58/Fort_Yuma%2C_Cal.-Fort_Yuma_Historic_Marker.jpg/250px-Fort_Yuma%2C_Cal.-Fort_Yuma_Historic_Marker.jpg)](/wiki/File:Fort_Yuma,_Cal.-Fort_Yuma_Historic_Marker.jpg)

**Fort Yuma Historic Marker**

* [![Fort Yuma Old Barracks](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Fort_Yuma%2C_Cal.-Fort_Yuma_Old_Barracks.-1870-_in_California.jpg/250px-Fort_Yuma%2C_Cal.-Fort_Yuma_Old_Barracks.-1870-_in_California.jpg)](/wiki/File:Fort_Yuma,_Cal.-Fort_Yuma_Old_Barracks.-1870-_in_California.jpg "Fort Yuma Old Barracks")

* [![Fort Yuma Southern Pacific Railroad Station](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/81/Fort_Yuma%2C_Cal.-Southern_Pacific_Railroad_Station-1877-2.jpg/250px-Fort_Yuma%2C_Cal.-Southern_Pacific_Railroad_Station-1877-2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Fort_Yuma,_Cal.-Southern_Pacific_Railroad_Station-1877-2.jpg "Fort Yuma Southern Pacific Railroad Station")

| * [v](/wiki/Template:Butterfield2 "Template:Butterfield2") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Butterfield2 "Template talk:Butterfield2") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Butterfield2 "Special:EditPage/Template:Butterfield2")  [Butterfield Overland Mail](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail "Butterfield Overland Mail") 2nd Division Stations | |
| --- | --- |
| * [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") – Located 12 miles southeast of Cahuenga Station in the [pueblo of Los Angeles](/wiki/Pueblo_de_Los_Angeles "Pueblo de Los Angeles"). The 2nd Division headquarters was in a brick building, consisting of an office, blacksmith shop, stables and sheds. * [Monte](/wiki/El_Monte,_California "El Monte, California") – Located 13 miles east of Los Angeles. * [Rancho San Jose](/wiki/Rancho_San_Jose "Rancho San Jose") – Located 12 miles east of Monte in present-day [Pomona](/wiki/Pomona,_California "Pomona, California"). * [Rancho Santa Ana del Chino](/wiki/Rancho_Santa_Ana_del_Chino "Rancho Santa Ana del Chino") – Located 12 miles southeast of Rancho San Jose. * [Temescal Station](/wiki/Rancho_Temescal_(Serrano)#Temescal_Butterfield_Stage_Station_and_Temescal "Rancho Temescal (Serrano)") – Located 20 miles southeast of Rancho Santa Ana del Chino. * [Laguna Grande Station](/wiki/Rancho_La_Laguna_(Manriquez)#Manriquez_Adobe.2C_Laguna_Grande_Butterfield_Stage_Station_site "Rancho La Laguna (Manriquez)") – Located 10 miles southeast of Temescal Station, near present-day [Lake Elsinore](/wiki/Lake_Elsinore "Lake Elsinore"). * Alamos or [Willow Springs Station](/wiki/Willow_Springs_Station "Willow Springs Station") – a later station, 11 miles south of Laguna Grande Station. * [Temecula Station](/wiki/Rancho_Little_Temecula#Apis_Adobe "Rancho Little Temecula") – Located 21 miles southeast of Laguna Grande Station. * [Tejungo Station](/wiki/Aguanga,_California "Aguanga, California") – Located 14 miles east of Temecula Station, near Aguanga * [Oak Grove Stage Station](/wiki/Oak_Grove_Butterfield_Stage_Station "Oak Grove Butterfield Stage Station") – Located 12 miles southeast of Tejungo Station. * [Warner's Ranch](/wiki/Warner%27s_Ranch "Warner's Ranch") – Located 10 miles southeast of Oak Grove Station. * [San Felipe Station](/wiki/Rancho_Valle_de_San_Felipe#San_Felipe_Station "Rancho Valle de San Felipe") – Located 10 miles southeast of Warner's Ranch, northwest of nearby [Scissors Crossing](/w/index.php?title=Scissors_Crossing&action=edit&redlink=1 "Scissors Crossing (page does not exist)"). * [Vallecito Station](/wiki/Vallecito,_San_Diego_County,_California "Vallecito, San Diego County, California") – Located 18 miles southeast of San Felipe Station. * [Palm Spring Station](/wiki/Palm_Spring_Station "Palm Spring Station") – Located 9 miles southeast of Vallecito Station at [Palm Spring](/wiki/Palm_Spring,_San_Diego_County,_California "Palm Spring, San Diego County, California"). * [Carrizo Creek Station](/wiki/Carrizo_Creek_Station "Carrizo Creek Station") – Located 9 miles east southeast of Palm Spring Station. * [Sackett's Wells](/wiki/Sackett%27s_Wells,_California "Sackett's Wells, California") – a later station, located 171⁄2 miles east southeast of Carrizo Creek Station, 15 miles west northwest of Indian Wells. * [Indian Wells Station](/wiki/Indian_Wells_Station "Indian Wells Station") – Located 32 miles southeast of Carisso Creek, near present day [Heber](/wiki/Heber,_California "Heber, California"), no water except at station. * [New River Station](/wiki/New_River_Station "New River Station") – a later station, located 15 miles southeast of Indian Wells Station, in [Baja California](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail_in_Baja_California "Butterfield Overland Mail in Baja California"), 14 miles west of Alamo Mocho Station, in present day [Mexicali](/wiki/Mexicali "Mexicali"). * [Alamo Mocho Station](/wiki/Alamo_Mucho_Station "Alamo Mucho Station") – Located south of the Mexican border in [Baja California](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail_in_Baja_California "Butterfield Overland Mail in Baja California"), 38 miles east of Indian Wells Station, no water except at station. * [Gardner's Wells Station](/wiki/Gardner%27s_Wells_Station "Gardner's Wells Station") – a later station, located south of the Mexican border in [Baja California](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail_in_Baja_California "Butterfield Overland Mail in Baja California"), 9 miles east of Alamo Mocho and 9 miles west of Seven Wells. * [Salt or Seven Wells](/wiki/Salt_or_Seven_Wells "Salt or Seven Wells") – a later well, located south of the Mexican border in [Baja California](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail_in_Baja_California "Butterfield Overland Mail in Baja California"), 18 miles east of Alamo Mucho. * [Cooke's Wells Station](/wiki/Cooke%27s_Wells_Station "Cooke's Wells Station") – Located south of the Mexican border in [Baja California](/wiki/Butterfield_Overland_Mail_in_Baja_California "Butterfield Overland Mail in Baja California"), 22 miles east of Alamo Mocho Station, no water except at station. * [Pilot Knob Station](/wiki/Pilot_Knob_Station "Pilot Knob Station") – Located 18 miles east of Cooke's Wells. * [Fort Yuma Station](/wiki/Jaeger_City,_California "Jaeger City, California") – Located 10 miles east of Pilot Knob Station.   Source: ["List of Butterfield Overland Mail Stations "Itinerary of the Route""](https://timesmachine.nytimes.com/timesmachine/1858/10/14/78877651.pdf) (PDF). New York Times. October 14, 1858. | |
